response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and systems engineer, but the profile does not align with this role's hard requirements. The position demands 8 years of C++ and deep large-scale cloud compute platform experience; the resume shows only a brief (~6 month) stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments plus a C++ bachelor thesis, far short of the bar. Distributed-systems exposure exists (low-latency micro-services at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby) but in DLang/Node rather than C++, and there is no internal cloud compute platform or formal cloud/DevOps technical-leadership experience. Strong engineering breadth and mentoring/teaching track record, but the core technical match is missing.
strengths: Real distributed-systems work: low-latency micro-services handling millions of HTTP req/s
Broad senior/staff-level experience and strong mentoring/teaching record (workshops, dojos, courses)
Owns and ships complex projects end-to-end across web, mobile, and embedded
concerns: C++ experience is roughly 6 months plus a thesis — far below the required 8 years
No internal cloud compute platform or large-scale cloud architecture experience
No formal technical-leadership role mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers

key_factors.factor: Cloud compute platform / cloud architecture experience
